rationale: The cited paper is used for a specific conceptual function in the citing paper's future-research discussion of whether their text-default prediction patterns might change once market participants learn those patterns. The citing paper explicitly invokes Van Heerde et al.'s Lucas-critique conditions (dissemination, incentive, ability to change behavior) to structure that argument, rather than listing it as generic background. Although this citation is peripheral to the main empirical model, it meaningfully informs interpretation of the study's external validity and dynamics over time.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited paper's Lucas-critique framework to assess whether borrowers and lenders might adapt after the findings become known. It uses the three-condition logic from the cited work to reason about when their predictive relationships may remain stable versus shift.
